expoparker.description: Thank you all so much for 1 million followers. I love you all #fyp

cleoabram.description: What would happen if you drilled a hole straight through the ice in Antarctica and jumped in? You jump in and the snow around you quickly changes to dense blue ice. As you go deeper, you realize the air bubbles trapped all around you are from the time of the Renaissance on the surface, then Ancient Rome, then the last Ice Age! But the pressure is rising and you’re getting closer to Earth’s hot core, the ice suddenly turns into… To find out what it is, follow along! #Antarctica #ice #mystery #science

What if you just keep going? Imagine you fly off at the speed of light. After 8.3 minutes, you’d reach our Sun. After 4 hours and 6 minutes, you’d pass the furthest planet in our solar system. How long do you think it takes you at the speed of light to get to the farthest object humans have ever sent? Follow if you think less than 24 hours. But at 4 years in, just as you’re passing our nearest star… something weird happens… If you like optimistic science and tech stories, follow for more of our show Huge If True! #animation #farthest #science #technology #historyfacts
These are the new Meta Ray-Ban Display AI Glasses. I got to test them out so here’s what they can do – and how they work. #MetaRayBanPartner #MetaRayBan Display @raybanmeta

This plane cured my fear of flying.  A Zero G flight is incredibly strenuous – not just for the occupants (though definitely them too, it’s called the Vomit Comet for a reason!) but also for the plane!  A Zero G flight requires 3 pilots to complete 31 maneuvers that involve repeatedly experiencing VERY steep inclines, HEAVY G forces, and casually diving toward the Earth.  I assumed these planes needed to be specially designed and reinforced to pull this off.  It turns out… I was wrong. Follow along to find out more! #ZeroG #airplane #Flying #Science #Technology

We’re about to see a supermoon.  Supermoons only happen a few times per year and they always occur consecutively. You might have spotted one in October and we’re about to see a few more.  But what really is a supermoon and what causes it? Here’s when you can catch the next ones! If you believe there should be more optimistic science and tech stories, follow along! #Science #space #moon #astronomy

This is the biggest black hole collision ever! And scientists just spotted it.  Two black holes with masses over 100 times our sun collided into each other billions of light years away…creating ripples in the fabric of the universe that we detected here on earth. To go inside the places that detected these ripples, follow along! #science #gravitationalwaves #blackhole

This asteroid might hit the moon and we might have to nuke it. No, seriously. Earlier this year, we thought this asteroid 2024 YR4 might be on track to hit Earth. Good news: It won’t. Bad news: it might now hit the moon. So, NASA just came out with a real proposal for what we could do if a lunar impact becomes more likely. Here are our options. To find out what happens next, follow along! #asteroid #NASA #moon #earth #follow

What’s the biggest asteroid to ever hit the Earth? Turns out the one that killed the dinosaurs WASN’T that big, at least compared to others that have hit our planet before. Here’s how they stack up… To find out how we’re protecting Earth, follow along! #asteroids #earth #space #science

How would you fake the stolen Louvre jewels? If the police find the stolen pieces from the Louvre, how will they know they’re real and not fake? Imagine you’re the thief and you’re trying to replicate the stolen tiara. What’s gonna get you caught is the pearls! Here’s why… That’s why I’m willing to bet we’ll find them… eventually. If you think so too… follow along! #science #louvre #jewels #diamond #pearl

What would happen if the sun just…. disappeared? At first, nothing! We’d keep orbiting the empty spot.  And we’re so far away that its final rays would take 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach us… And then… DARKNESS But the scariest part is: What would happen to the Sun’s gravity?  Follow for more stories like this! #sun #science #gravity #earth

Why does this plane need 3 pilots? Zero G flights are very difficult to pull off, particularly for the pilots. In order to make everyone on the plane feel weightless the pilots need to keep the plane perfectly around you. That means mastering a physics problem that requires three separate pilots. Here’s why. Follow along for more! #science #technology #airplane #animation

What’s the biggest planet ever found in our whole observable universe? Our first contender is huge. You could fit 23 Earth’s across it!! This planet is less dense than marshmallows. But if you’re good with a little more scientific uncertainty, our second contender looks like it’s even bigger… Then there’s the last contender… If you think there should be more optimistic science and tech stories, follow along! #science #shorts #planet #biggest #space

What if you just kept shrinking? By the time you’re smaller than an ant, you could safely jump off a chair – air resistance lets you glide down. But bad news, your voice is so high that no human can hear you anymore! Plus, insects are monsters! Now, smaller, cells are huge next to you! Going smaller, now you’re being bombarded by air molecules You go inside an atom and you find, there’s a surprising amount of empty space here. If you go small enough and somehow keep the same mass… whoops, sorry… If you believe there should be more fun, optimistic science and tech stories, follow along! #shorts #shrinking #science #smaller #quantum

Breaking news, this image could show life on Mars (really)… It was taken by the Perseverance rover in a place that was likely filled with water but dried up over time. The rover took a sample of the rock and here’s what it found… To find out what happens next, follow along! #mars #science #space #NASA

This comet smashed into Jupiter.  This happens a lot, and it might have saved your life. Jupiter is called the “vacuum cleaner of space” because of its unique ability to “clean up” our galaxy. Its massive size pulls passing objects into its orbit, sometimes shredding them to pieces and sometimes launching them out of the solar system entirely. Its remarkable ability to capture asteroids has prevented plenty of them from reaching us here on Earth. But the jury is still out on if this planet is actually our cosmic ally, because… If you believe there should be more optimistic science and tech stories, follow along!  #jupiter #comet #asteroid #space

What is 3I/ATLAS really? I keep hearing about this ancient alien object speeding toward our Sun… that some scientists say might be “alien technology”. So I emailed one of the world’s most important astrophysicists, Dr. Alex Filippenko, to learn what’s real. And no, it won't hit us… but there are nearby asteroids that might! We’re working on a story all about Near Earth Objects.  To find out how we protect our planet… follow along!   #comet #asteroid #space #alien

This is the first 3D image of a human embryo implanting itself. You remember, egg meets sperm to make an embryo and then it attaches itself to the lining of the uterus where it gets the nutrients to grow! But this only goes right about 40% of the time, making it one of the biggest roadblocks in reproduction. So to get a better look, scientists created a gel that mimics the uterus, and watched how a mouse embryo and a human embryo latched on… For more optimistic science and tech stories, follow along! Scientists found at: https://x.com/ojosnegros_sam?lang=es #science #3D #tech #animations

How would we stop a dangerous asteroid headed toward Earth? Option 1: You do nothing. There’s always a chance it misses us! Option 2: You SMASH A SPACECRAFT INTO it to change its course JUST ENOUGH that it misses Earth. Option 3... And there are two more wilder options as well. To see those, watch our longer video with Mark Rober on YouTube and follow along! #science #asteroid #space #earth #animation

This experiment saved kids’ lives.  In 1941 two researchers ran an experiment on some kids in Philadelphia. They hung ultraviolet “irradiation chambers” in some classrooms. The devices emitted ultraviolet light around the top of the room, above the heads of the children. They were testing to see how effective UV light was at killing germs - then a measles outbreak swept US cities. Their results were shocking. #measles #UV-C #FarUV #Science #tech

I’ve been cheering at the wrong moment during a rocket launch.  Liftoff is incredibly cool, but for the engineers, they’re still anxious, because it’s just the start. A big test is about to happen around a minute in, at a point called “Max Q” And this is why it’s important, so next time cheer with the engineers! If you think there should be more optimistic tech stories, follow along!  #rockets #launch #space #engineer

Can you drink sewage water? This guy is about to! Here’s how… There are lots of ways of getting people clean drinking water and Team Water is trying to give 2 million people clean water for decades. So I’m doing a 1 hour call with one donor!! Go get it! 1 dollar is one year of clean drinking water for someone who needs it.  #teamwater #water #science #tech

These super common LED lightbulbs almost didn’t exist…  You need 3 colors to make white light. But one was missing. Why? It has do to with how the electrons “dance”...  #LEDs #technology #light #discovery #animation

What if you just kept holding your breath? Do not try this at home. The official record for holding your breath and no air moving is… But with pure oxygen? Follow along if you think it’s longer than 20 minutes! This is Huge If True, our optimistic show about science and technology. #science #oxygen #breathing #recordbreaking #technology

This is the largest piece of Mars we’ve ever found on earth! It contains a rare glass created only under the intense shock wave pressures from an asteroid impact. We think this piece was ejected off Mars by an asteroid and sent hurtling towards Earth… But how do we know it’s from Mars and not somewhere else? Also…someone just bought it for $5.3 MILLION… For more optimistic science and tech stories follow along! #science #Mars #asteroid #meteorite #space

The world’s most famous robot dog has a new trick. You all remember Spot, the viral robot dog from Boston Dynamics. Well, now he can do backflips! But why does that matter? And how did it learn? Turns out it’s surprisingly important… For more optimistic science & tech stories, follow along! #robot #technology #bostondynamics #science #stem

THREE people can make a baby. Eight new babies in fact... Scientists fertilized eggs with sperm, but then removed the nucleus of the cell, where both parent’s DNA was, and put it into a donor embryo with a THIRD person’s mitochondrial DNA. Here's why...  Follow for more optimistic science and tech stories. #science #dna #ivf #egg

How smart are chimps really? This chimpanzee is using a stick to eat termites! This is huge because for a long time we thought that only us humans made and used tools… until Jane Goodall carefully studied chimps in Tanzania in 1960. She revealed they were much more complex than we had ever thought, teaching us so much more about our closest relatives, and ourselves. Thank you, Jane Goodall. We’ll miss you. #chimpanzees #science #janegoodall #animals

Let’s play a game! All of these animals lived with the dinosaurs. And then…the asteroid hit. Here are three match-ups, can you guess which of the two animals survived? If you got them all right, subscribe! These visuals were from the American Museum of Natural History’s newest exhibit - go check it out! #science #AMNH #asteroid #dinosaurs #animation

3I/ATLAS is still zooming through our galaxy and capturing everyone’s imagination. Early reports are in about what it’s made of. But how do we know without physical samples? Turns out… You can learn a lot from light. Here’s how and follow along to learn more! #comet #spectroscopy #space #interstellar #3iatlas

A brand new medical treatment seems to have saved this little boy’s life. Out of the 20,000 genes in his body, one has a rare mutation, causing a life-threatening disorder. To help, scientists took stem cells from his blood, then used a harmless virus to insert a new working gene… For more optimistic science and tech stories, follow along! #science #medicine #gene #tech #shorts
